title: Support for Unicode in PGS Connector
breadcrumbs: Support for Unicode in PGS Connector
text: Support for Unicode in PGS Connector The Uniface PGS connector for PostgreSQL supports the full Unicode range. To enable Unicode support in the connector, set the assignment setting $DEF_CHARSET= UTF8. This ensures that the Uniface C packing code is mapped to W packing code. This enables Unicode characters to be used in table names and column names, in the SQL Workbench, and with the sql ProcScript statement. Important: For PostgreSQL, you must ensure that the $DEF_CHARSET= UTF8 is set for all applications that access the data via the PGS connector. If it is set to different character sets, it can corrupt the data. When modeling application data, use wide-character packing codes (such as W20) for fields. W packing codes are stored as UTF-8 and support the full Unicode range. By default, Unicode support for W packing codes is limited to characters in the Basic Multilingual Plane (BMP). You can enable storage of the entire Unicode range (Supplementary Multilingual Planes) by setting the smp connector option. For more information, see smp . For or more information, consult the PostgreSQL documentation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
